title: What Does Being Older Bring That Being Younger Didn't?
subtitle: A journal prompt for finding what ageing has given rather than only what it has taken
excerpt: A journal prompt for turning the question of ageing around: not what has been lost, but what has arrived. For use when the dominant feeling about getting older is subtraction.

practice.whenToUse: Use when getting older feels primarily like subtraction, or when you want to build a fuller picture of what each decade actually contains.

content.text: The dominant cultural story about ageing is one of subtraction: what the body loses, what opportunities close, what youth offered that age cannot. This prompt inverts the question. It asks what the years have actually brought in the other direction.
Set a timer for fifteen minutes. Write without stopping. Begin with the prompt below.
What does being older bring that being younger didn't?

If you get stuck, try these: What do I care about less than I used to? What do I know about myself now that I did not know at 25? Where have I stopped trying to be something I am not? What relationship with my own opinions have I developed? What has got easier? What am I less afraid of?
The gains of ageing are real but they tend to be quieter than the losses. They need to be named deliberately. This prompt is the naming.
